evidence: Our research and development efforts are strategically centered on expanding our leadership within the enterprise security industry through AI-powered innovation.
Our investments in research and development, including investments in AI, may not result in design or performance improvements, marketable products, subscriptions, or features.
The rapid evolution of AI...requires us to invest significant resources to develop, test, and maintain AI in our products and services.
Cortex XSIAM automates data integration, analysis, and triage to respond to most alerts, enabling analysts to focus on only the incidents that require human intervention.
Prisma AIRS is a comprehensive AI security platform...providing AI model scanning, posture management, red teaming, run-time security, and AI agent security.

In fiscal 2025, we began to transform our hiring operations by strategically embedding AI across the talent acquisition lifecycle...AI-generated job descriptions, structured interview guides...intelligent interview scheduling.
AI algorithms may have flaws, and datasets used to train models may be insufficient or contain biased information.
These efforts could subject us to regulatory risk, legal liability, including under legislation regulating AI in jurisdictions such as the E.U.
Developing, testing, and deploying AI systems may also increase the cost profile of our offerings due to the nature of the computing costs involved in such systems.

limitations: ai_investment_intensity: No quantified dollar amounts, GPU/data-center scale, or headcount figures tied to AI investment appear in the excerpts; R&D spend totals are not quoted. Score 3 reflects named AI infrastructure and dedicated engineering teams but no quantified scale.

ai_narrative_concreteness: Several named products are described as deployed and in production (Cortex XSIAM, Prisma AIRS, SCM, AIOps) with operational detail, but no quantified business results (e.g., % improvement, dollar savings, volume handled) appear in the excerpts, capping the score at 4.
capex_mentions: No explicit dollar or quantity figures tied to AI/compute/data-center investment were found in the excerpts.
